summary: The average number of youth in ACS's Close to Home juvenile-justice placements has more than doubled since Fiscal 2022. ACS attributes this to a rising number of youth with complex cases requiring longer, more intensive rehabilitation, combined with discharge delays tied to housing shortages and a lack of family support — factors that keep youth in placement longer even in periods when new admissions weren't rising as fast.

sources.label: ACS, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing as complex cases and housing shortages delay discharges
